summary: Iter17 — order cancel/refund + reorder banner + horizontal categories + ChefHat + responsive grid. Backend 12/12 pytest pass. Frontend critical flows verified end-to-end against the public preview URL.
tests_executed: BACKEND POST /api/restaurant/orders/{id}/cancel: 401 unauth, 404 not-found, 403 cross-user, 400 for status in {preparing,delivered,cancelled,out_for_delivery}, 200 happy-path with refund_amount==total credited to wallet_balance, wallet_transactions credit row with 'cancellation refund' reason, response shape {ok, order, refund_amount, wallet_balance} — ALL PASS
BACKEND regression: GET /restaurant/orders, POST /restaurant/order, /restaurant/verify, /restaurant/orders/{id}/track, /restaurant/menu — ALL PASS

FRONTEND /restaurant/orders: order-cancel-{id} button visible only for status=paid (absent for delivered) — PASS. Click triggers window.confirm; on accept, API called, order chip flips to CANCELLED, '₹150 refunded to wallet' line appears under Total, cancel btn removed — PASS
FRONTEND /restaurant/track/{id}: track-cancel-btn ABSENT for delivered + cancelled orders — PASS (only 'paid' state would show it; cancelled order had btn absent as expected)

FRONTEND Restaurant page reorder-banner: visible when user logged in AND has at least one delivered order; reorder-banner-cta + reorder-banner-dismiss present; dismiss sets sessionStorage 'efc_reorder_dismissed_v1'='1' and banner stays hidden across reload — PASS
FRONTEND restaurant-categories: container has overflow-x-auto (horizontal sticky chip rail), 6 chips rendered including cat-all — PASS
FRONTEND restaurant-items grid classes = 'grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-2.5 sm:gap-3' — PASS
FRONTEND BottomNav for logged-in subscriber: bottom-nav-restaurant present, ChefHat icon (lucide-chef-hat) confirmed in inner SVG — PASS

BACKEND wallet credit verified directly in mongo: wallet_balance=150.0, wallet_transactions credit row with reason='Restaurant order cancellation refund · rorder_…', restaurant_orders.refund_amount=150 / refund_mode='wallet' — PASS

ui_bugs.issue: After successful cancel + refund, the header wallet badge still shows the stale ₹0 until a page reload or auth context refresh. The DB wallet_balance is correctly updated and the order card itself renders '₹150 refunded to wallet', so this is purely a client-side stale-state issue (cancelOrder() does not call refreshUser()/auth.refresh).

action_items: (LOW) After successful cancel in RestaurantOrderHistory.jsx (and OrderTrack.jsx), invoke the AuthContext refresh (e.g. await refreshUser() or call /api/auth/me) so the header wallet pill reflects the new balance immediately without requiring a manual reload.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/routes/restaurant.py L385-411 (customer_cancel_order): correctly guards on status=='paid', uses $inc for wallet_balance (idempotent-safe at the DB level), logs a wallet txn with a clear reason, and returns the canonical {ok, order, refund_amount, wallet_balance}. Status check before mutation gives natural idempotency: 2nd call returns 400 ✅

Status set is 'paid' only — note that some orders may transition through internal states ('preparing','out_for_delivery','delivered') without ever surfacing back to 'paid', so the 400 message ('kitchen has already started or it has been delivered') is accurate ✅

Open question / nice-to-have: cancellation refund credits to wallet only, no Razorpay refund call is made even for live (non-mock) Razorpay payments. Current spec explicitly says wallet-only refund — flagging here so future iterations are aware that real-money customers will get a wallet credit, not a card refund. This is intentional per problem statement.

Frontend Restaurant.jsx reorder-banner trigger uses the FIRST delivered order in the last 10 (api.get('/restaurant/orders?limit=10').find(o.status==='delivered')), which since the list is newest-first means 'most recent delivered'. Correct ✅
BottomNav.jsx label 'Restaurant' produces data-testid 'bottom-nav-restaurant' (lowercase) — matches spec ✅

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the spec'd flows; 1 LOW UX nit (stale wallet badge until reload)

test_credentials: Used direct mongo session-token seeding (TEST_st_*) per /app/auth_testing.md — bypasses OTP rate limits and lets the test use a deterministic auth cookie. All TEST_* users + sessions + orders + wallet_transactions cleaned up at end of run.
seed_data_creation: Per-test isolated TEST_subscriber users with auto-cleanup (module fixture). Frontend test seeded one TEST_fe_* user with one paid order + one delivered order (cleaned up after run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: 1) Use mongo session-token seeding (see test_iter17.py _seed_user) — much faster + more reliable than OTP send-otp/verify-otp loops, no rate limits. Set the cookie via page.context.add_cookies with domain (NOT url) on this preview host. 2) For Playwright clicks on small icon buttons inside a flex row (e.g. reorder-banner-dismiss X icon, order-cancel-{id} ghost button next to Reorder/Track), prefer page.evaluate("() => document.querySelector(SEL).click()") over page.click(force=True) — the latter occasionally lands on the wrong element on mobile viewport (390x844). The JS .click() fires the real React handler reliably and is what we used for the cancel/dismiss confirmations. 3) Always register page.on('dialog', d=>d.accept()) BEFORE clicking buttons that call window.confirm. 4) For verifying horizontal scroll layout, check container has 'overflow-x-auto' class — there are 6 categories on the live menu (All + 5 cats).
rca of the issue: No bugs found in the under-test feature set. The single LOW item is a UX polish gap (stale wallet pill in header until reload) — not a functional bug; backend wallet is correctly updated and the order card itself surfaces the refund line.
